title: Meenakshi Temple, Shiva, Nataraja, Ananda Tandava, Shaivite sanctum sanctorum, Madurai, India.jpg
source_url: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Meenakshi Temple, Shiva, Nataraja, Ananda Tandava, Shaivite sanctum sanctorum, Madurai, India.jpg
description: Ananda Tandava is a cosmic dance performed by the Hindu deity Shiva in his manifestation as Nataraja, the Lord of Dance. It symbolizes the continuous cycle of creation, preservation, and dissolution (death). Agni fire is the energy of destruction and renewal. The dissolution paves the way for renewal. The dance breaks the boundaries and helps dissolve the illusions of the mind and the universe.This Chola-era bronze sculpture must be very old as it resides in a 14th century Shaivite sanctum sanctorum.  Meenakshi Temple. Madurai, Tamil Nadu, India.
